person.name: Robert de Mohaut

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
This profile is about the second Robert to be hereditary dapifer (steward or "sewer") of the Earls of Chester. His brother Ralph had first inherited it from their father.

«b»Complete Peerage summarizes what is known like this:«/b»

ROBERT DE MOHAUT, br. and h. Between 1192 and 1208, as lord of Mold and Steward of Chester, he made a grant of Goosetrey to the abbey of St. Werburgh, and about 1208-1211 he confirmed to the abbey the gift of Neston church "as the charter of Ralph my brother attests." He was engaged in the "great War" (maxima guerra) with Llewelyn, 1198-99. He granted pasture rights in Saltney to Basingwerk Abbey. He d. circa 1210.

Farrer's Honors and Knights Fees Vol II, a source for Complete Peerage, goes into a bit more detail. Note that the Chartulary of Chester Abbey is "Tait" in CP, and the plea mentioned is the one from Bracton, mentioned above:

In 1187 payment for 7 days was made for 13 archers whom Robert de Mohaut retained in the king's service. Robert is named in 1208 in a Leicestershire plea. He was brother of Ralph, as appears by his Neston charter, cited herein, and succeeded him as sewer, so that he was probably heir. But the record of a plea as to the advowson of Cretingham church, given at n.106 below, gives the succession thus: Ralph (temp. Henry I),  s. Robert I,  s. Ralph,  s. Roger I, son and heir in 1222. Robert II seems to be omitted, but the second Ralph has been altered from Robert, so that the omission of 'bro. Robert' may be suspected at that point. Thus Roger I would be son of Robert II, which would agree with his statement that his father was engaged in the maxima guerra with Llewelyn (1198-9). The point, however, must be left undecided. Robert de Mohaut, sewer of the earl of Chester, confirmed the grant of the church of Neston to Chester abbey according to the charter of his brother (f'ris) Ralph, by deed attested by Philip de Orreby, justice of Chester, and issued between 1208 and 1211. Roger de Mohaut II appears to have repudiated the grant, but afterwards released the advowson to the abbey. Robert de Montalt, seneschal, granted rights in Saltney to the monks of Basingwerk, Philip de Orreby, justice of Chester, being a witness. Robert's death occurred during the period of 1208-1212.

In 1212 it was presented from Calceworth wapentake that Roger de Mohaut, the seneschal (of Chester), held 1 fee in that wapentake of the earl of Chester;

So in summary:

He was probably an adult by 1187, and certainly by 1198, possibly starting his young adult life as a soldier while his elder brother Ralph held their father's inheritance. His brother made a grant without him about 1180, at which time Robert was perhaps not yet an adult. So Robert was born about 1165.
His brother Ralph, to whom he was heir, died about 1200-1207.
Robert himself died in the period 1208-1212, so was probably dapifer for only a few years.
The connection between this generation, Ralph and Robert II, and the next, Roger, is not certain. Complete Peerage thinks it likely he was Robert's son, but he could for example be a nephew or perhaps even their younger brother (as proposed by Ormerod)? That they did have a younger brother named Roger is not doubted because he was mentioned in a charter.

«b»Family«/b»
Robert possibly had no wife, and possibly had no issue.

A possible son is Roger, who took over his offices and lands.
A possible daughter is Beatrix, concubine of David de Malpas.
In his Montalt essay, but not in his history of Cheshire, Ormerod argues that Robert must have never married because there is no mention of her dower in an inquisition into dowers of the family from the time of Edward I. This seems to ignore the possibility that he had a wife who predeceased him.

An older but also imperfect authority for Roger being son to Robert is a visitation pedigree put together by the 16th century herald Robert Glover.

This late and imperfect authority is apparently also the only source of the information that Robert had a daughter Beatrix who was "concubine" to David de Malpas.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Mohaut於1165年出生在威爾士福林爾哈沃登時，他的父親Robert de Monte Alto66歲，母親Leucha FitzNigel34歲。他與Agnes de Lacy結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1210年享年45歲。

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Mohaut was born in 1165, in Hawarden, Flintshire, Wales, his father, Robert de Monte Alto, was 66 and his mother, Leucha FitzNigel, was 34. He married Agnes de Lacy. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1210, at the age of 45.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Mohaut(이)가 1165년 웨일스 플린츠히르 호르든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Monte Alto(은)는 66세였고, 그의 어머니 Leucha FitzNigel(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Agnes de Lacy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1210년 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Mohaut родился в 1165 г. в местности хаварден, флинцхир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Robert de Monte Alto было 66 лет, а матери, которую звали Leucha FitzNigel, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes de Lacy. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1210 г. в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Mohaut nació en 1165, en Hawarden, Flintshire, Gales, su papá, Robert de Monte Alto, tenía 66 años y su mamá, Leucha FitzNigel, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Agnes de Lacy. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1210, a la edad de 45 años.
